raw_consumer_statement:
Fraudulent Bar Charge Dispute with Barclays XXXX Submitted Fake Receipt and Made False Statements I am filing a formal complaint regarding a fraudulent {$300.00} charge that was placed on my Barclays credit card by a bar owner, and the subsequent mishandling of my dispute. 

On XX/XX/year> I visited XXXX XXXX and was charged {$200.00}, which I agreed to and do not dispute and then another {$300.00}, an amount that I did not authorize and which does not reflect the cost of my purchases. I was taken advantage of while under the influence of alcohol, and the bar owner failed to provide an itemized receipt, nor was I made aware of the charge at the time. 

Upon discovering the unauthorized charge the following morning, I immediately disputed it with Barclays. However, during their investigation, the vendor submitted a receipt for exactly {$300.00}, which I never signed, and no itemized receipt was provided. It is highly suspicious that the total would come out to exactly {$300.00}. 

Moreover, the vendor falsely claimed they did not notice that the receipt wasnt signed, implying that this was an oversight on my part, which is an absolute falsehood. This is clearly an attempt by the vendor to mislead Barclays and avoid taking responsibility for their actions.
